free_text: Radioisotope yields from 1.85-GeV protons on Mo and
1.85- and 5.0-GeV protons on Te
Roanoke College,Salem,Virginia
Experiment performed at the Lawrence
Berkeley National Laboratory's Bevatron accelerator
Gamma rays from each of the targets were
counted separately with 100 cm3 coaxial HPGe
detectors inside a 5-cm thick lead shielding
Spallation products were identified
according to their characteristic decay gamma rays
Polymethyl methacrylate sheets in a stacked sample
were used to monitor the integrated beam exposure
through the production of 11C. 11C yield was
determined from coincident 511-511 keV events detected
by annular segmented NaI detector.
Systematic uncertainty arising from the
overall normalization of the cross section
Statistical uncertainty
Compiled by S.H.
OS. Some isomer assignments corrected in
subs. 2 and 3.
Data taken from tab. 3 of the reference
Comment by compiler: 7.8 min activity was assigned by
authors to Nb-88g.
Comment by compiler: 14.3 min activity was assigned by
authors to Nb-88m.
Comment by compiler: 2.0 hr activity was assigned by
authors to Nb-89m.
Natural Mo target with diameter of 3.1 cm and
thickness of 0.67mm. The sample was assembled in a
stack with polymethyl methacrylate sheets (C5O2H8)n,
which served as monitor. The irradiation was performed
performed with the targets in air.
Isomer assignments for Nb-88 and Nb-89
corrected in DECAY-DATA and ISOMER
Data taken from tab. 4 of the reference
Wrong half-life in paper, see comment for 108mIn
Wrong half-life in paper, authors interchanged in
tab. 2 half-lives of 108mIn and 108gIn. According to
NuDat, correct values are 108gIn 58.0min,
108mIn 39.6 min. (Comment by compiler)
Wrong half-life in paper, see comment for 110mIn
Wrong half-life in paper, authors interchanged in
tab. 2 half-lives of 110mIn and 110gIn. According to
NuDat, correct values are 110gIn 4.9hr,
110mIn 69.1 min. (Comment by compiler)
Two samples were used. At 1.8 GeV a natural
Mo target with diameter of 3.1 cm and thickness
of 0.67mm was used. For the 5 GeV irradiations
a block with an area of 5.1 x 5.1 cm and thickness
of 1 cm was used. Each sample was a assembled in a
stack with polymethyl methacrylate sheets (C5O2H8)n,
which served as monitor. The irradiation was performed
with the targets in air.
Isomer assignments for In-108, In-110 and
Sn-123 corrected in DECAY-DATA and ISOMER
